title: phyteuma michelii subsp. betonicifolium
intro: Phyteuma michelii subsp. betonicifolium, also known as a subspecies of the alpine cotton grass, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and other compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and supporting the immune system. Phyteuma michelii subsp. betonicifolium is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
